---
title: "scRNAseq analysis"
---

***

### Overview

This pipeline transitions raw count matrices into biologically annotated cell clusters using Scanpy. We employ robust quality control and an iterative clustering strategy (Rounds 1-3) to progressively refine the level 1 cell clusters. We additionally generate fine-grained subclusters for well represented level 1 subclusters and genenerte pseudobulk counts for all level 1 and level 2 cell populations.

***

### Workflow Logic

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    classDef snazzy fill:#f1f1f1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px,color:#000;

    A[(Plate 1-3 H5AD)] --> B(scanpy_qc)
    B --> C{Clustering R1}
    C --> D{Clustering R2}
    D --> E{Clustering R3}
    E --> F[scanpy_annotation]
    E --> G[scanpy_pseudobulk]
    E --> H[scanpy_subclustering]
    
    class A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H snazzy
    style F fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
    style G fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
```

1. `scanpy_qc`: Per-plate quality control (Mito %, Gene/UMI counts).
2. `Iterative Clustering`: Three rounds of dimensionality reduction (PCA/UMAP) and Leiden clustering to identify and remove doublet-driven or low-3. quality clusters.
3. `scanpy_annotation`: Expert-led cell-type labeling.
4. `scanpy_pseudobulk`: Aggregates single-cell data into pseudo-replicates for eQTL mapping.

***

::: {.callout-note}

Per-rule Jupyter notebook

Every rule produces a static HTML report with summary QC output.
:::

***

### Resources

| Rule | Threads | RAM (MB) | Walltime | Logic |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| `scanpy_qc` | 16 | 160,000 | 3h | Per-plate parallel execution |
| `scanpy_clustering (1-3)` | 16 | 380,000 | 3d | Iterative cluster refinement |
| `scanpy_annotation` | 10 | 200,000 | 1h | Cell-type labeling |
| `scanpy_pseudobulk` | 10 | 100,000 | 1d | Sum-aggregation for eQTL |
| `scanpy_subclustering` | 8 | 80,000 | 2h | Generate suscluster populations |

***
